channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: 007 First Light is getting new story content later this year
item.content:encoded:
IOI is not holding up with 007 First Light. The game may have only just released but during Summer Game Fest a brand new story mission was teased for later this year. There is very little information on what this new mission will entail, but it has been set up by Bawma, who plays a part in the 007 First Light.

In our 007 First Light Review, Jim said, “007 First Light is an incredibly well-executed action adventure blockbuster with a truly engrossing story, slick presentation, and layered gameplay systems. It marks a major evolution for IO Interactive, thrusting the studio into that coveted top tier of video game developers. It also has us excited for the team’s future projects, whether that’s more Bond or a return to Hitman at some point down the road.”

Source: Summer Game Fest



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/05/007-first-light-is-getting-new-story-content-later-this-year/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: SAW Genesis brings the franchise to the aftermath of World War 1
item.content:encoded:
SAW Genesis is a new game set in the SAW universe, this time set just after World War 1.

“SAW: Genesis allows you to play on either side of a 3v1 confrontation in intense matches that take place on procedurally generated maps,” say developers Bloober Team. “Step into the role of the Judge, an early architect of Jigsaw’s philosophy, manipulating the trial and its prisoners from behind the scenes. Or play as one of three Accused looking to escape the deadly labyrinth through teamwork and sacrifice.”

You will be able to play as The Judge who “is dedicated to rehabilitating humanity through pain and sacrifice”, or one of the three Accused.

The Judge can set traps and use perks, controlling the game from the shadows by using secret tunnels and noise detection. He also has hallucinogenic gas and paralyzing toxins, and “an Accomplice that can be summoned to drag the Accused into Rehabilitation Traps.” However, unlike other asymmetrical games, he does not posses greater strength than the accused and cannot take them down with force alone.

The three accused must work together to escape from the trap-laden maze. Your will be gradually drained, and if it gets to low, you may get ensnared in a trap, forcing you to decide between waiting for your team mates to rescue you – or sacrifice part of your body.

“SAW: Genesis is built for short, intense matches where no two trials play out the same,” say Bloober. Features include:

  • Procedurally generated levels that maximize replayability.
  • Dilemmas and puzzles inspired by the SAW franchise.
  • Rehabilitation traps with meaningful, lasting consequences.
  • A unique power dynamic where the Judge controls the game from the shadows, but the Accused can find moments to fight back with smart coordination and physical force.

The game is coming to Early Access on Steam very soon.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/05/saw-genesis-brings-the-franchise-to-the-aftermath-of-world-war-1/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company will launch on August 27th
item.content:encoded:
At Summer Games Fest, EA and Bit Reactor have confirmed when players can get their hands on the XCOM like Star Wars Zero Company, with the new entry into the series dropping on August 27th for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series XS. Alongside the confirmation of the date, a brand new trailer was unleashed showing what to expect. General Skywalker is one of those you should expect.

Star Wars Zero Company is a turn based tactics game set during the twilight of The Clone Wars, and it follows Hawks, a former Republic Officer, and his group known as Zero Company. All of the Zero Company members are essentially mercenaries for hire from across the galaxy, with each of them having their own skills. You have a droid that will be to able to hack systems, a Jedi that has Force abilities, a Mandalorian armed with all the gadgets, and a Clone Trooper. That isn’t all as these characters are fully customisable, and you will be able to create your own squad members to create a team that adheres to your playstyle. Each of the main characters has their own story which you may learn about if your bonds deepen.

Bit Reactor, the team behind Star Wars Zero Company could be well be creating a spiritual successor to XCOM and that is no light conclusion. Bit Reactor is made up of developers that have worked on the XCOM games, so we would expect to see some of that influence and learning to be present within Star Wars Zero Company.

Source: Summer Game Fest



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/05/star-wars-zero-company-will-launch-on-august-27th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads is the next game in the fighting series
item.content:encoded:
SEGA has revealed Virtua Fighter Crossroads, the next entry in the Virtua Fighter series. The brand new trailer shows the Chinese mafia out for blood but Pai Chan comes in to save the day. The reveal trailer shows what looks like some gameplay, but it is primarily cinematic. Catch it or yourself below.

SEGA has stated that there will be a full Virtua Fighter Crossroads broadcast June 5th at 5:00 PM PDT 8:00 PM EDT/ June 6 at 12:00 AM UTC which will give a lot more information on what to expect in the game, with we’d assume a proper look at the gameplay. A release date is yet to be confirmed but it will be at some point in 2027.

Source: Summer Game Fest



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/05/virtua-fighter-crossroads-is-the-next-game-in-the-fighting-series/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars 3 has been revealed with a gorgeous trailer
item.content:encoded:
ArenaNet has been teasing an announcement at Summer Game Fest, with rumours going around that it would be Guild Wars 3. Now, it can be confirmed that ArenaNet was indeed teasing Guild Wars 3! The announcment comes just shy of 14 years since Guild Wars 2 was released, though that has received six major expansion packs, with the last being Visions of Eternity, which launched last year.

Launching on PS5 and PC the beta will start late in 2027, so there’s going to be a while until the finished game is on the cards.  You should be able to register on guildwars3.com but it does not seem to be live at the time of writing.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/05/guild-wars-3-has-been-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars 3 Is an Action/Adventure MMORPG Prequel Slated for PC and PlayStation 5 with Closed Beta in Fall 2027
item.content:encoded:
Following the recent teases, ArenaNet took the stage at Summer Game Fest 2026 to unveil Guild Wars 3, the next iteration in the MMO saga.

The developers are calling it an action-adventure MMORPG, a modern evolution of the genre that blends rich action combat, character building, and skill collection. In Guild Wars 3, players will master a combat system where depth is achieved through strategic skill use, positioning, and movement, thus mixing classic action RPG combat with Guild Wars-style build-making and creating a unique combat experience. Another trademark feature of the game is a movement system that transfers your momentum between modes of travel, allowing players to run, slide, leap, and bound across the environments.

In the press release, ArenaNet reveals that this is actually a prequel set a thousand years before the events of the original game. It's set in the Tyrian region of Orr, a vast wilderness frontier imbued with the world’s magic. Nature entities with strong connections to the land, called Vael spirits, embody the vitality of this lush, verdant landscape. Various guilds are engaged in conflict over how to protect or exploit the bounty beyond civilization’s borders. These spirits will vary in size and influence within the ecosystems around them, with the most notable of these being the Seeker. As seen in the trailer, every player’s Seeker will serve as a connection to the spirits of Orr and a mount that will carry them on their journeys through the game’s open world.

In Guild Wars 3, players are a Vaelwarden, a guardian of the land of Orr, protector of the people and spirits that call Orr home. They'll discover untold stories and forge lasting bonds with their fellow players as they glide, leap, sprint, and ride across a frontier full of adventure around every corner.

Colin Johanson, Guild Wars 3 Game Director and ArenaNet Studio Head, said in a statement:

Guild Wars 3 is a new era not just for ArenaNet and Guild Wars, but also for MMORPGs as a whole. The MMORPG genre has millions of players worldwide eager for new, modern experiences, and the opportunity to harness that excitement is immense. Our approach with Guild Wars 3 is to push MMORPGs forward, to create an online game world that feels believable, rewarding, responsive, and innovative while at the same time respecting players’ time and providing a positive, supportive space to build community and enjoy new stories in our universe.

The new game will be the first to land on consoles, with GW3 planned for a PC and PlayStation 5 launch and full controller support. The Steam page is already live and includes the minimum PC system requirements, though there's a decent chance they are preliminary and subject to change:

OS: Windows 10 64-bit
Processor: Intel Core i5-8400 or AMD Ryzen 5 2600
Memory: 16 GB RAM
Graphics: RTX 2060 / Radeon RX 5700 / Intel Arc A580
Storage: 65 GB available space

The trailer ended with the reveal that Closed Beta will begin in Fall 2027, thus suggesting a full launch could come in 2028. Pre-registration should be available via the official website once it stops being hammered.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/guild-wars-3-announced-pc-ps5/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tupac Shakur joins Snoop Dogg in Stranger Than Heaven, which also has a release date once you've finished getting over the whole dead rapper cameo bus
item.content:encoded:
Shakur's likeness is being added to the game with permission from his estate

Right. So. Stranger Than Heaven, the Like A Dragon/Yakuza devs' hop through different decades of 20th century Japanese history? You're probably already aware that Snoop Dogg's in it. Well, a digitally resurrected version of fellow rapper Tupac Shakur's also in it. For reasons. Reasons that I'm sure will be readily apparent when Stranger Than Heaven comes out in January 2027.

The Tupac cameo and that release date of January 15th, 2027, have just been announced by RGG executive director Masayoshi Yokoyama at Summer Game Fest. Snoop was also there to explain that Tupac, shown in a trailer Yokoyama introduced, is appearing with his estate's blessing.

Notably, he's not the only dead bloke confirmed to be starring in Stranger than Heaven so far. Japanese actor Bunta Sugawara, who died back in 2014, is also appearing in the game with his family's consent. Shakur, meanwhile, died in 1996, about nine years before the first Yakuza game came out.

Please welcome Masayoshi Yokoyama from RGG Studio, visiting the U.S. for the first time in two decades! He’s here with a new Stranger Than Heaven announcement, joined by @SnoopDogg and Cordell Broadus! #SummerGameFest pic.twitter.com/Bs8lPFQchz

— Summer Game Fest (@summergamefest) June 5, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

I'll be honest, as with far too many of RGG's casting decisions of late, I'm feeling a bit icky about this one. If it's just Shakur's appearance being adopted by a character, as is the case with Sugawara, then it'll be alright, if more than a bit surreal. If there's any AI voice gubbins, though, I'll be thoroughly put off.

Walking corpses aside, I'm intrigued by Stranger Then Heaven's story, which tells the tale of half Japanese/half American protagonist Makoto Daito eventually founding the Tojo Clan - the famous yakuza group Kazuma Kiryu belongs to - following a bunch of band management and brawling in different decades. For a fuller picture of the story, check out my article from May.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/tupac-shakur-joins-snoop-dogg-in-stranger-than-heaven-which-also-has-a-release-date-once-youve-finished-getting-over-the-whole-dead-rapper-cameo-business
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Paramount’s Among Us Series Is Out Today, But Don’t Call An Emergency Meeting To Watch It
item.content:encoded:
It’s been a couple years since we heard anything about the Paramount+ Among Us animated series, and you would have been forgiven for thinking it had gotten lost somewhere in the vents. But surprise, all 10 episodes are out today, featuring a stacked celebrity voice cast but little in the way of substance or hearty guffaws.

Among Us the series is exactly what you think it is: it follows a colorful crew of little Among Us dudes stuck on a spaceship together with an alien shapeshifter posing as one of them and murdering them, one by one. There’s roughly one murder an episode, with the crew frantically trying to find the imposter in between deaths while the audience is similarly left in the cold dark of space until (presumably) the end.

I’ve seen the first five episodes so far. While strong personalities aren’t integral to the game, the show does a good job of distinguishing each color dude and giving them a cursory amount of motivation and personality. Patton Oswalt is one of the standouts as White, a stuck-up rich guy who won a contest for a space ride, Yvette Nicole Brown is solid as Orange, the deeply corporate-loyal head of HR, who in tandem with White and the young, naive, unpaid intern Green (Elijah Wood) gets in a few good but shallow zingers at the expensive of billion-dollar corporations and capitalism. My favorite in the cast is Randall Park as Red, the deeply incompetent captain whose bumbling attempts at unifying the group and looking cool led to most of the chortles the show managed to get out of me. I don’t mean to spoil things so early, but one of these four dies fairly early on, and I find it ridiculous to hire them for this and then not keep them around for very long.

Which wasn’t a lot, to be clear. I don’t think Among Us the show is bad. It’s fine! There are some good gags, especially if you pay attention to the art and animation detail. It is also deeply predictable: I know from the jump which characters are going to last until the final few episodes, and the first half of the series contained no surprises to debunk that prediction. If you’re watching this to see a long string of references to the game, you’ll be perfectly happy. The comically gruesome deaths showing a single bone in the weird crew bodies, the big Emergency Meeting button, the pizza, all the different rooms, security cameras, vents, it’s all there. It’s mostly referential fluff. It is essentially just like watching some celebrities play Among Us without you. I feel like I’d rather just get my friends together and play the game.

Friends or no friends, the other announcement in this segment was for Among Us Story: On Guard, a new story-driven Among Us game that’s about to be available to be wishlisted on Steam. No release date here, though we’ll likely get more details once the Steam page is up.

Anyway, if after watching several hours of Summer Game Fest, you want to wind down by watching a few episodes of Among Us (which are all very short, in the ballpark of 12-15 minutes apiece), all 10 of ’em are on Paramount+ right now.



https://kotaku.com/paramount-among-us-series-out-today-2000702494
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: It’s Finally Time For Guild Wars 3
item.content:encoded:
Guild Wars 3, bay-bee!

Yup, it’s real. Guild Wars is getting its third incarnation, as announced at Summer Game Fest today. It’s planned for a Steam and PS5 release sometime in the future, and there’s a beta beginning in fall 2027. You can register to get a chance to enter the beta at the official website once it goes live (likely any minute now).

We don’t know much else about what’s coming in Guild Wars 3, just that it’ll be another MMORPG and will continue to take place in the fantasy world of Tyria. Onstage, ArenaNet studio head Colin Johanson said that the new Guild Wars would focus on the “joy of movement” and that its combat would “usher in an entirely-new type of MMO experience.” We have no idea what that means just yet, but given how heavily movement mechanics featured in Guild Wars 2, this could be something pretty cool.

Guild Wars 2 has been going since August of 2012, so there’s no arguing that it’s a good time to move to a new version. Since its launch, it’s seen six expansions: Heart of Thorns, Path of Fire, End of Dragons, Secrets of the Obscure, Janthir Wilds, and Visions of Eternity. While it’s never quite reached the heights of popularity that World of Warcraft and Final Fantasy XIV have seen, Guild Wars 2 has remained stable and successful for over a decade now, with continuously positive critical response and, blessedly, no monthly subscription cost.

Honestly, this might pull me back into Guild Wars when the time comes. I’m a one-MMORPG-at-a-time girlie and World of Warcraft is my poison of choice, but I love the Sylvari, the dynamic Guild Wars 2 combat system, and the lovely world of Tyria. 2027 is far enough away that I can clear my schedule, right?



https://kotaku.com/its-finally-time-for-guild-wars-3-2000703153
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Virtua Fighter Crossroads Revealed At Summer Games Fest
item.content:encoded:
The next Virtua Fighter game has been officially revealed, and it’s a large open-world experience.

During Summer Games Fest 2026, Virtua Fighter Crossroads was announced and showed off its open-world concept in a massive trailer.

Cielo was featured prominently in the trailer, showcasing single-player gameplay that looked much more like Yakuza than a traditional Virtua Fighter. The game will release in 2027, but a full release date was not shown. More details will be shown at an official showcase for the game later this evening at 8:00 p.m. ET.

The Story will be updated in time.

Check out everything announced here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

Star Wars Zero Company Receives Gameplay Premiere During Summer Game Fest

The first gameplay trailer for Star Wars Zero Company was revealed during Summer Game Fest, as well as an official release date.

SAW: Genesis Announced During Summer Game Fest

SAW: Genesis, a 3 vs 1 survival horror game based on the SAW franchise, has been announced during Summer Game Fest.

End of Abyss Release Date Revealed

The release date for End of Abyss has been revealed by Section 9 Interactive at Summer Game Fest 2026. Here are more details.

Gundam Rogue Orbit Release Window Revealed

A new Gundam game called Rogue Orbit was revealed by Bandai Namco at Summer Game Fest. Here are more details.



https://insider-gaming.com/virtua-fighter-crossroads-revealed-at-summer-games-fest/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company Receives Gameplay Premiere & Release Date During Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
A world-first look at gameplay was shown for Star Wars Zero Company during Summer Game Fest, with a release date confirmed for August 27, 2026.

Star Wars Zero Company was confirmed to appear at the Summer showcase prior to the event, and the trailer was as impressive as expected.

Gameplay Details For Star Wars Zero Company

In case you were unfamiliar, Star Wars Zero Company is a new single-player turn-based tactics game developed by Bit Reactor, who have built a team that’s worked on franchises like XCOM and Civilisation; to say they’re masters of the genre would be an understatement.

Set in the twilight of the Clone Wars, you’ll assume the role of Hawks, a fully voiced and customizable protagonist in charge of the intelligence unit. Alongside them, you’ll build a ragtag team ready to fight the separatist forces, including the likes of battle droids, droidekas, and a whole host of Star Wars aliens.

The new trailer showcased the XCOM-centric gameplay, and if you’re a fan of the franchise, you’ll feel right at home with Star Wars Zero Company. With a deeper narrative, risk of permadeath, and a large-scale war to fight, the game looks like a treat for Star Wars fans looking for a gritty Clone Wars adventure. Oh, and Anakin was showcased—what more do you need?!

Check out the new gameplay trailer for yourself below:

This was just one of the many announcements during Summer Game Fest. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

What do you think of Star Wars Zero Company? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the drop off for Nintendo Switch 2 sales in Japan, as well as when the Steam Machine is set to ship.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/star-wars-zero-company-receives-gameplay-premiere-release-date-during-summer-game-fest/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: End of Abyss Release Date Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Section 9 Interactive has revealed at Summer Game Fest 2026 that End of Abyss will release in October of this year.

End of Abyss is a single-player action-adventure developed by Section 9 Interactive and published by Epic Games. The developers have finally confirmed a release date for the title: End of Abyss arrives on October 1, 2026. Interested readers can pre-order the game now on XBOX Series X/S and PC.

About End of Abyss

“End of Abyss” is a game that puts you in the role of a combat technician named Cel. As you explore the derelict areas of a mysterious underground facility, you will gradually uncover the secrets of its origins while battling some of the most dangerous monsters. Players must investigate disturbances of unknown origin that spiral deeper into an abandoned world as they navigate the compound.

The journey will require players to upgrade their gear and adapt to the environment as they progress. There are various branching paths and hidden areas that can be revealed using your Scanner and other tools. These pathways will become accessible once players gain more power, allowing them to revisit forgotten corridors and uncover secrets that were previously sealed away.

In other news, a new Gundam game called Rogue Orbit was revealed by Bandai Namco at Summer Game Fest. Additionally, Paramount Games has revealed a new Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles title called The Last Ronin. What are your thoughts on End of Abyss releasing in October 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Guild Wars 3 being announced at Summer Game Fest. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

Gundam Rogue Orbit Release Window Revealed

A new Gundam game called Rogue Orbit was revealed by Bandai Namco at Summer Game Fest. Here are more details.

Guild Wars 3 Announced During Summer Game Fest

Guild Wars 3 has been announced during Summer Game Fest 2026, with it being the first new title in the MMO franchise in over a decade.

Crossfire Announced During Summer Game Fest

Crossfire, a new third-person narrative adventure, has been officially revealed during Summer Game Fest.

Paramount Games Announces New Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Game

Paramount Games is here. Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles is owned by the Paramount Global Corporation. Now, a new TMNT game is in the works.



https://insider-gaming.com/end-of-abyss-release-date-revealed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gundam Rogue Orbit Release Window Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco revealed a brand new game for one of the most popular sci-fi franchises out there, with Gundam Rogue Orbit arriving in 2027.

The reveal started with what looks like a cinematic trailer, with Gundam finally coming to save the day. However, things quickly changed as the trailer transitioned from cutscene moments to proper gameplay, with the Gundam taking on proper colossal creatures across different environments.

The trailer begins with a shot of sunflowers swaying in the wind, which quickly escalates into chaos. A massive creature appears to be attacking a space station, and viewers are shown various types of armored robots attempting to confront it. However, none of the robots succeed in their mission, leading Gundam to step in and confront the creature to restore order.

The new trailer provided players with a glimpse of the story behind the upcoming title, but the standout features were definitely the gameplay mechanics. Viewers got to experience the various types of attacks and weapons that players will have at their disposal, with a massive sword being highlighted prominently. Additionally, the trailer offered insights into the types of enemies players will face, which appear quite formidable. Check out the trailer for Gundam Rogue Orbit below:

In other news, Guild Wars 3 has been announced during Summer Game Fest 2026, with it being the first new title in the MMO franchise in over a decade. Additionally, Crossfire, a new third-person narrative adventure, has been officially revealed during Summer Game Fest. What are your thoughts on Gundam Rogue Orbit’s reveal trailer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about gen ATLAS being revealed at Summer Game Fest 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Multiplayer horror game SAW: Genesis announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Coming soon to Early Access.

Publisher Bloober Team and developers Broken Mirror Games and Anshar Studios have announced SAW: Genesis, a three-versus-one multiplayer horror game. It will launch first in Early Access for PC via Steam.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Set in the grim aftermath of World War I, SAW: Genesis allows you to play on either side of a 3v1 confrontation in intense matches that take place on procedurally generated maps.

Step into the role of the Judge, an early architect of Jigsaw’s philosophy, manipulating the trial and its prisoners from behind the scenes. Or play as one of three Accused looking to escape the deadly labyrinth through teamwork and sacrifice.

I Want to Play a Game: Rise as the Judge

Shaped by their experiences in The Great War, the Judge is dedicated to rehabilitating humanity through pain and sacrifice.

Customize your approach with perks and traps, then seek to control the match from the shadows. Unlike other asymmetrical multiplayer games, the Judge is physically vulnerable and cannot overpower the Accused through sheer force alone.

Build your strategy around your character’s distinct advantages, such as:

  • Exclusive access to hidden corridors and a noise-detection map.
  • Hallucinogenic gas and paralyzing toxins that distract and immobilize the Accused.
  • An Accomplice that can be summoned to drag the Accused into Rehabilitation Traps.

Your goal is to rehabilitate the Accused, making them choose between life and limb rather than killing them. Live or die. They make their choice.

Welcome to Your Rebirth: Become the Accused

The three Accused find themselves in a trap filled environment, where only collaboration and their will to live can give them a chance at survival.

Coordinate with teammates to complete challenges, scavenge items, and collect keys before time runs out. Each match drops you into a procedurally generated map where routes shift, plans break, and every mistake gives the Judge a greater advantage.

Your greatest threat isn’t just the traps that could be around every corner – it’s the pressure you face as time runs out. As your will decreases, progress becomes more difficult, and eventually you become vulnerable to becoming ensnared in one of the Judge’s intricately engineered rehabilitation traps, forcing you to decide between:

  • Sacrificing a body part to escape.
  • Relying on your teammates to rescue you in time.

These choices have permanent consequences for the match. Damage your arm and it will be harder to complete puzzles and fight back. Injure a leg and you’ll move slower, becoming easier to corner.

Survival is possible, but you may not survive intact.

Think You Will Walk Away, Untested?

SAW: Genesis is built for short, intense matches where no two trials play out the same. Over the course of the game, you’ll experience:

  • Procedurally generated levels that maximize replayability.
  • Dilemmas and puzzles inspired by the SAW franchise.
  • Rehabilitation traps with meaningful, lasting consequences.
  • A unique power dynamic where the Judge controls the game from the shadows, but the Accused can find moments to fight back with smart coordination and physical force.

Make a choice. Pay the price. Let the games begin.

Watch the announcement trailer below. Visit the official website here.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/multiplayer-horror-game-saw-genesis-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Grounded 2 Early Access coming to PS5 on August 11 alongside ‘Into the Abyss’ update
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

More players are about to enter Brookhollow Park.

Publisher Xbox Game Studios and developer Obsidian Entertainment will release the the Early Access version of Grounded 2 for PlayStation 5 on August 11 alongside the game’s “Into the Abyss” update, the companies announced.

Grounded 2 first launched in Early Access for Xbox Series and PC via Steam and Microsoft Store on July 29, 2025.

Watch a new trailer below.

PlayStation 5 Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/grounded-2-early-access-coming-to-ps5-on-august-11-alongside-into-the-abyss-update
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: RuneScape: Dragonwilds launches September 15 for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Currently in Early Access for PC.

Open-world survival crafting game RuneScape: Dragonwilds will leave Early Access and launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on September 15, the company announced. The PlayStation 5 version was announced earlier this week and will be available via the PlayStation Plus Game Catalog, and the Xbox Series version was announced for the first time today.

RuneScape: Dragonwilds first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on April 15, 2025.

“Our community has been at the heart of RuneScape: Dragonwilds from day one,” said RuneScape: Dragonwilds executive producer Jesse America in a press release. “Every update throughout Early Access has been shaped by player feedback, and we’re incredibly proud of what we’ve built together. Launching 1.0 this September is a huge milestone for the team, and we’re excited to continue growing RuneScape: Dragonwilds with players for years to come.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Jagex:

About

et them through battles, but find their practical uses as well, as they collect items and craft things to help them throughout their journey. In this land riddled with fierce dragons, players must level up skills, prepare potions, and craft gear, as they carve a path through these ferocious beasts and make their way to the Dragon Queen herself.

Key Features

  • RuneScape at Its Core! – Embark on quests, level skills while exploring a beautiful, hand-crafted world. This all-new adventure blends high-fantasy and iconic RuneScape lore on an undiscovered continent. Battle legendary RuneScape creatures like Zogres, challenge Black Knights and the entirely new and fearsome Garou, all set to a sweeping soundtrack of reorchestrated RuneScape classics and hours of brand-new music.
  • Grind Skills, Slay Dragons – Locate them, throw down the gauntlet, and carve a path to the Dragon Queen herself. Follow the trail of destruction and uncover the mysteries of the continent to learn not just how to confront the dragons, but how to overcome them.
  • Survival Through Sorcery – Summon a spectral axe to fell trees in an instant! Blink away from danger in a cloud of magic. Turn bones into… peaches? The Anima-rich landscape of Ashenfall offers a power unlike any other. Conjure a tornado of fish from a lake! Explode ore veins with a snap of your fingers! Journey through the vibrant regions of Ashenfall, discover why wild magic spills from the fractured lands and harness that power to survive!
  • Community Driven, Player Centric – Like all RuneScape games, community is at the heart of RuneScape: Dragonwilds. Whether players are adventuring with friends or going it alone, every opinion matters, and it’s up to the community to help shape the future of our game.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/runescape-dragonwilds-launches-september-15-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gundam Rogue Orbit announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

"High-mobility action game" due out in 2027.

Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Bandai Namco Studios have announced Gundam Rogue Orbit, a “high-mobility action game that delivers a bold new take on Gundam.” It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam in 2027.

Here is an overview of the game, via Bandai Namco:

About

High‑mobility action game that delivers a bold new take on Gundam through fast, cinematic combat in an all-new universe.

Key Features

  • Become the Pilot You Were Born to Be – Step into the cockpit as the pilot of Gundam Helix and join a rag‑tag crew of unsung soldiers as humanity faces a powerful unknown threat.
  • Fight Your Way Through Fast and Intense Battles – Take control and jump into fast and hard-hitting melee combat. Every fight rewards skill, mastery, and quick, decisive action as you push your Gundam to its limits.
  • Your New Gundam Experience Begins Here – The cinematic story of GUNDAM ROGUE ORBIT is a daring new chapter in the GUNDAM saga, built as the perfect entry point for new players and a thrilling evolution for longtime fans.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/gundam-rogue-orbit-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Paramount Games Studio and PlatinumGames announce Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin for consoles, PC
item.content:encoded:
Black Forest Games version no longer in development.

Publisher Paramount Games Studio and developer PlatinumGames have announced Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin, an “AAA action adventure game” based on the comic book series. It will be available for unspecified consoles and PC.

A Paramount Games Studio representative told Gematsu that the version of the game developed by Black Forest Games and published by THQ Nordic, which was announced in 2023, is no longer in development.

“Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin was in development by Black Forest / THQ Nordic, but before Skydance merged with Paramount, development had stopped,” a Paramount Games Studio representative told Gematsu. “Knowing how much fans were anticipating the game, Paramount Games Studio decided to continue development and opted for PlatinumGames, as they stood out as the partner that truly understood their vision for The Last Ronin.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Paramount Games Studio:

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin is a AAA action-adventure game being developed by Platinum Games and published by Paramount Games. Based on the popular comic book series, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin follows the last surviving Ninja Turtle as he embarks on a desperate mission for vengeance. Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Last Ronin will be available on consoles and PC.

Watch the announcement teaser trailer below.

Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/paramount-games-studio-and-platinumgames-announce-teenage-mutant-ninja-turtles-the-last-ronin-for-consoles-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin's Creed Creator Reveals Witchcraft Action Game 1666: Amsterdam
item.content:encoded:
Patrice Désilets and Panache Studios have revealed their second game, 1666: Amsterdam. This new action game lets players control a witch in the titular city.

Players control Noa Brooklyn, who is living in Amsterdam, which is inhabited by entities called The Originals. These long-living creatures have accumulated and abused their power, but it's up to Noa to take that power back due to her role as a hunter of sorts called a Collector. 

To do that, Noa uses magic to expose and destroy the demonic Originals in an open-world Amsterdam. The Originals resemble normal humans, so it takes a keen eye and plenty of magical tricks to locate them. Noa will have help from 300 years in the future in the form of Aaron, a person hailing from 1999 who is transported back in time and occupies the body of a black cat. And yes, it looks like you can control Aaron in his feline form. 

Désilets took the Summer Game Fest stage to reveal that the game is launching into Early Access later this year, and that players can download a free playable prologue today on Steam and the Epic Games Store. 



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/assassins-creed-creator-reveals-witchcraft-action-game-1666-amsterdam
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Zero Company, The Single-Player Tactics Game, Gets August Release Date And Anakin Skywalker In New Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars Zero Company, the single-player turn-based tactics game coming from Star Wars Jedi: Survivor developer Respawn Entertainment and Bit Reactor, launches this August. More specifically, it will launch on August 27 on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC, narrowly avoiding the September onslaught of games, which includes Game Informer cover story game The Blood of Dawnwalker, Marvel's Wolverine, Control: Resonant, and more.

This release date was revealed in a new gameplay trailer that aired during the Summer Game Fest livestream today, giving us our best (and first real) look at the latest Star Wars game yet. It also gave us our first look at Anakin Skywalker in the game. Considering Bit Reactor, which is a studio formed by industry veterans who worked on XCOM and more, is one of the primary developers behind it, it's no surprise that it looks like an exciting tactics adventure in the beloved science-fantasy universe. 

Check it out for yourself below in the Star Wars Zero Company release date trailer: 

In Star Wars Zero Company, players control Hawks, a former Republic officer, who commands a ragtag group of mercenaries and bounty hunters from across the Clone Wars-era galaxy to combat a dangerous new threat. Hawks' class and appearance are fully customizable, and the teammates you recruit will come in the form of classic Star Wars characters, including Clone Troopers, astromechs, Jedi, and more. And yes, the teammates can be customized, too. 

Player choice will shape both the narrative and gameplay, as you increase bonds between squadmates to unlock new abilities and powerful synergies. Outside of combat, you can build a base and send out intelligence informants to learn your enemy's plans and more. 

Again, Star Wars Zero Company hits PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC on August 27, 2026. 

In the meantime, check out these screenshots from Star Wars Celebration Japan, and then check out the original Star Wars Zero Company reveal trailer. 

Are you picking up Star Wars Zero Company on day one? Let us know why in the comments below!



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/star-wars-zero-company-the-single-player-tactics-game-gets-august-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars 3 Announced For PC And PS5 With A Beta Coming Next Year
item.content:encoded:
Guild Wars 2 came out nearly 14 years ago, and after a decade and a half of support and expansions, the team is pushing out a proper sequel. Guild Wars 3 was announced at Summer Game Fest this evening, and developer ArenaNet is calling it the modern evolution of the MMORPG. Check out the reveal trailer below:

"Explore Tyria like never before in Guild Wars 3, an action-adventure MMORPG and modern evolution of the genre," the trailer's description reads. "Play solo or form a band of adventurers, forging impactful and enduring bonds with fellow players, a formative cast of memorable NPCs, and the nature spirits that call Orr home."

While the teaser doesn't show us any concrete gameplay, it does show off a stunning, vibrant art style, which we hope will translate over to the full release. The announcement also came with the news that the game will be coming to both PC and PS5, which is a significant move for a series that has typically avoided console releases.

You'll be able to get your hands on the game in Fall 2027, when the beta begins. For more information, the trailer points viewers towards guildwars3.com. It's also available to be wishlisted on Steam.

For more coverage of Summer Game Fest 2026, head to gameinformer.com/sgf



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/guild-wars-3-announced-for-pc-and-ps5-with-a-beta-coming-next-year
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Fully Commits To Pod Racing In Its SGF Trailer
item.content:encoded:
To date, Star Wars: Galactic Racer has only teased pod racing, but now it is officially podracing. The Summer Game Fest trailer introduces the planet Domenikos shows plenty of podracing, and confirms that Sebulba will be in the game.

The trailer also teased all kinds of new characters and lots of story content, though beyond attempting to become a legendary racer, it's not clear exactly what  the story is about.

We also finally learned the release date: October 6.



https://gameinformer.com/sgf-2026/2026/06/05/star-wars-galactic-racer-fully-commits-to-pod-racing-in-its-sgf-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The next Virtua Fighter finally got a proper name and a fancy trailer at Summer Game Fest
item.content:encoded:
Sega's next big Virtua Fighter game finally has a proper name, which has been revealed alongside a fancy new trailer.

Virtua Fighter Crossroads is set to release in 2027. The trailer we got showed off what looks to be a story mode for the game, where the player character can be seen navigating the world and getting into fights. How this will actually play isn't quite clear, but it's an enticing look at the game nonetheless.

Until now, all we saw of this new Virtua Fighter was some awesome looking one-vs-one fighting. Which, y'know, is what you'd really want from a new Virtua Fighter. Without a set release date in place, one hopes this trailer is the start of a steadier rollout of character reveals and extended looks as we jiggle our way towards its launch.

Virtua Fighter Crossroads, first announced in January of last year, instantly won the hearts of old school fighting game fans. It also is quite the looker, with this new trailer only emphasising that point.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/virtua-fighter-crossroads-trailer-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Guild Wars 3 announced at Summer Game Fest, beta releasing in Fall 2027
item.content:encoded:
Guild Wars 3 has been announced at Summer Game Fest. It will be playable through a beta come fall 2027.

The MMORPG will be an "evolution" of the MMO genre, according to ArenaNet's CEO Colin Johanson who spoke a few words on stage. The trailer itself shows some open world navigation, with a character running up a a sheer cliff face.

Developed by ArenaNet, the game is a sequel to the beloved Guild Wars 2, which remains a popular online game roughly 14 years after its release.

It's described as having comabt that will "usher in an entirely new type of MMO experience", with movement powered by momentum. It seems like it could be a major deviation from the typical MMORPG affair Guild Wars 2 is, but that's not a huge surprise considering how much of a leap Guild Wars 2 was away from the original Guild Wars.

The game has been long speculated due to a string of hirings at ArenaNet, all for unnamed, unannounced projects. In addition, ArenaNet has been pumping a lot more money into marketing, having released a trailer for the current Guild Wars 2 expansion at a major game showcase, and putting out an excellent Guild Wars documentary.

This is quite the bombshell reveal, it's safe to say. You can wishlist Guild Wars 3 now, and sign up for a newsletter on the Guild Wars 3 website.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/guild-wars-3-announcement-summer-game-fest
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
